anomaly_probe.anomaly_candidate: Caregiving, medicine, teamwork, school, transport, and wage labor create shared deadlines that are not merely borrowed comparison clocks.
anomaly_probe.confidence_effect: Keeps the idea at extended. It can only survive by sharply separating coordination obligations from status comparison.
anomaly_probe.why_it_breaks_or_strains_model: Some external clocks are obligations of justice, safety, and trust. If path language lets a person dismiss medication times, childcare pickup, workplace dependencies, or promises, the model becomes harmful and false at the practice level.

claim_units: A clock measures repeatable transitions along a worldline.
There is no single cosmic now that ranks all lives as early, late, ahead, or behind.
The felt now is where records have reached this body and action remains possible.
Clocks should coordinate obligations without becoming metaphysical or social verdicts.
The modern use case is time anxiety, digital comparison, burnout, and feeling late in life.

cross_domain_prediction.prediction: Teams using dependency-aware local timelines, with explicit interface commitments, will show less false lateness blame and better bottleneck diagnosis than teams managed only by global comparison dashboards.
cross_domain_prediction.structural_translation: A distributed system has local process histories and coordination protocols, not one privileged global now. The practical question is which timestamps mark real dependencies and which merely create misleading rank comparisons.
cross_domain_prediction.would_count_against_it: A single global deadline dashboard predicts delays, responsibility, morale, and coordination failures as well as or better than dependency-aware local timelines.

exact_claim: A clock measures path-dependent physical change along one worldline, not a universal river of time. The felt now is local enough for conduct and responsibility, and modern suffering worsens when another person's clock becomes a verdict on one's own life rather than a tool for coordination.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: People trained in the distinction report less behind-in-life and comparative shame language while keeping equal or better punctuality, promise-keeping, and planning behavior.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: The owed-coordination versus borrowed-comparison distinction has practical value.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Randomized pilot with deadline-stressed students, caregivers, founders, and knowledge workers.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Missed obligations increase, avoidance rises, or outcomes are no better than self-compassion, time management, or ordinary social-clock psychoeducation.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Students introduced to worldlines and proper time before block-universe diagrams will make fewer claims that relativity proves the present is fake or that there is one hidden cosmic schedule.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Physics education experiment with pre-post misconception coding.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Misconception rates match standard lessons, or the path framing worsens physics accuracy.

falsifiable_predictions.expected_observation: Digital comparison exposure and perceived social-clock deviation predict behind-in-life distress after controlling for actual deadlines, workload, income, age, and caregiving load.
falsifiable_predictions.if_model_is_right: Behind-in-life suffering is partly driven by borrowed social clocks rather than workload alone.
falsifiable_predictions.test_surface: Survey and diary study using social comparison, social-clock deviation, workload, and distress measures.
falsifiable_predictions.would_weaken_or_falsify: Distress is fully explained by material overload, insecurity, discrimination, sleep loss, or clinical anxiety, with no added effect from comparison clocks.

literature_search_queries: Einstein 1905 synchronism time clocks light signals
Minkowski Space and Time world-line proper time clock
Stanford Encyclopedia Being and Becoming modern physics local becoming proper time worldline
Rovelli partial observables time relational clocks
Page Wootters Evolution Without Evolution time correlations subsystems
Neugarten social clock early late on time life events
Elizabeth Freeman chrononormativity productivity time bodies
Hartmut Rosa social acceleration shrinking present deadline pressure

near_neighbors.difference: Einstein does not turn clock locality into a doctrine about comparison, responsibility, or feeling late in life.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the physical claim to known.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for operational clock time, clock synchronization, and moving-clock comparison.
near_neighbors.source: Einstein, On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies, https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/On_the_Electrodynamics_of_Moving_Bodies_(1920_edition)

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds a conduct and social-comparison interpretation.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Major downgrade for the phrase clock measures a path.
near_neighbors.overlap: World-points and world-lines already frame physical histories as paths in spacetime.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate applies local becoming to spiritual-practical pressure around life schedules.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade. The local-now physics and philosophy are already mapped.
near_neighbors.overlap: Extremely close. Stein, Arthur, Dieks, and Savitt already connect proper time, worldlines, local becoming, now-points, and causal diamonds.
near_neighbors.source: SEP, Being and Becoming in Modern Physics, https://plato.stanford.edu/entries/spacetime-bebecome/

near_neighbors.difference: They do not imply that another person's clock should not become a verdict on one's life.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades the anti-river and anti-cosmic-clock portion, while leaving room for an applied synthesis.
near_neighbors.overlap: Both support relational or internal-clock approaches in which time is not an independent universal flow.
near_neighbors.source: Rovelli, Partial Observables, https://arxiv.org/abs/gr-qc/0110035, and Page and Wootters, Evolution Without Evolution, https://cds.cern.ch/record/143641

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate speaks of my path or another path, which Dogen may treat as a reifying translation.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Anomaly and partial near-neighbor. It supports local answerability but strains path-self language.
near_neighbors.overlap: Dogen's being-time already resists external time and treats each being or moment as time, not as a thing located inside time.

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate connects social clocks to relativity and proper time.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Strong downgrade for the behind-in-life insight.
near_neighbors.overlap: Very close for the human-condition claim. Social clock theory already explains early, late, and on-time self-evaluation against culturally prescribed life schedules.
near_neighbors.source: Neugarten social clock theory, via The Time of Our Lives, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3894849/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate translates these pressures through clock-path language rather than queer theory or critical theory.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Downgrades social critique novelty. Remaining contribution is a practical distinction, not a discovery.
near_neighbors.overlap: Chrononormativity and social acceleration already analyze time as productivity discipline, shrinking present, schedule pressure, and embodied social regulation.
near_neighbors.source: Elizabeth Freeman, Time Binds, UC Davis summary, https://lettersandsciencemag.ucdavis.edu/bookshelf/time-binds, and Hartmut Rosa, Social Acceleration, Columbia University Press, https://cup.columbia.edu/book/social-acceleration/9780231148351/

near_neighbors.difference: The candidate adds proper-time path measurement and social comparison language.
near_neighbors.novelty_impact: Internal originality is reduced. This should be cross-indexed rather than treated as standalone.
near_neighbors.overlap: Already argued that clocks coordinate action without owning metaphysics and addressed deadline pressure.

next_loop_instructions: Do not publish as a new physics claim. Name proper time, local becoming, social clock theory, chrononormativity, and social acceleration as prior art.
Merge or cross-index with Clocks Do Not Own Time unless a separability test shows independent predictive value.
Build a practice rubric that separates owed coordination, chosen commitment, structural coercion, and borrowed comparison.
Test for harm by tracking missed obligations, avoidance, and contempt for shared time.
Interview physicists, social-clock researchers, burnout clinicians, caregivers, and team leads for concrete failure and repair cases.
Keep the teaching narrow: clocks coordinate paths, but they do not rank the worth of lives.
novelty_adjustment: Lower novelty from 0.50 to 0.34. Human-condition fit is direct but already well-covered by social clock theory, chrononormativity, social acceleration, burnout, and perfectionism literature. Target cohort: functioning adults who meet obligations but feel late in life because of visible peer timelines. Non-fit cases: acute crisis, severe depression, mania, psychosis, addiction withdrawal, people avoiding duties, and people facing structural exploitation where schedule pressure is materially imposed rather than internal comparison.

practitioner_test.answers_that_reduce_originality: Physicists call the physical claim textbook proper time, social scientists call the distress claim social clock or chrononormativity, and practitioners say the distinction does not change repair or instruction.
practitioner_test.answers_that_support_contribution: Practitioners can use the distinction to reduce comparative shame while preserving obligations, and can predict which deadlines are owed coordination versus status-contaminated comparison.
questions: Is clock measures a path just standard proper time and local becoming?
Does borrowed comparison time add anything beyond social clock theory, chrononormativity, or social acceleration?
Can you name cases where distinguishing coordination from comparison would change guidance?
What failure would show that path language is excusing avoidance or structural injustice?
relevant_practitioners: Relativity teachers and philosophers of time
Soto Zen teachers familiar with Dogen Uji
Developmental psychologists who study social clock theory
Clinicians working with burnout, perfectionism, and achievement-contingent self-worth
Caregivers, team leads, and operations managers responsible for real coordination
Meditation teachers who work with time anxiety

primary_pair: Einstein, On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies, section 1, https://en.wikisource.org/wiki/On_the_Electrodynamics_of_Moving_Bodies_(1920_edition)
primary_text_close_read.risk_of_overread: Moderate to high. Proper time is a precise physical concept, while a life path is a metaphor. The model may confuse social comparison with real coordination duties, and Dogen may reject the self-path language because it separates a person from being-time.
primary_text_close_read.secondary_literature_gap: The audit relied on SEP for Stein, Arthur, Dieks, Savitt, and local becoming. It did not read their full books and papers, full Neugarten originals, Freeman's full Time Binds, or full Hartmut Rosa. No physicist, developmental psychologist, or practitioner interviews were run.
primary_text_close_read.structural_observation: Einstein defines time through clocks, local readings, light-signal synchronization, and moving-clock comparison. Minkowski then gives world-points and world-lines as the natural spacetime structure. The candidate's physical core, a clock measures along a path, is therefore close to standard proper-time and worldline thinking rather than a new model.

title: A Clock Measures A Path
unlike_statement: Unlike Einstein, Minkowski, local becoming, Rovelli, Page-Wootters, Dogen, social clock theory, chrononormativity, and the prior Lumenary clock record, this idea's possible contribution is a testable practical distinction: separate owed coordination times from borrowed comparison times, then see whether that reduces behind-in-life distress without weakening punctuality or responsibility.
